sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Scott, a 28-year-old male, presented to the clinic with a right knee injury that occurred 3 days ago while playing soccer.
He reported a sudden pop in the knee and subsequent swelling, which has partially resolved.
He describes the knee as feeling limited in function and having pain on a scale of 6-7/10, which is slightly improved with ibuprofen use.
He has been able to bear weight on the knee but experiences pain and instability.
He reports a marked limp on the right leg and has difficulty bending and straightening the knee, with pain and stiffness.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen for pain relief.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has significant effusion in the right knee with slight ecchymosis.
Palpation at the medial joint line elicits pain.
Passive range of motion is limited, with 115 degrees of terminal extension and 20-30 degrees of terminal flexion due to pain.
The Lachman exam is positive.
Muscle strength is 5/5 in both lower extremities, and neurovascularly, the bilateral lower extremities are intact.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's right knee injury is suggestive of an anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) tear.
Problem list: ACL tear; suspected.
Plan:
I ordered an MRI to evaluate the integrity of the ACL.
I prescribed meloxicam 15 milligrams daily to reduce swelling and pain.
I advised the patient to ice the knee as needed and to moderate their activities, including wearing a knee immobilizer and using crutches when walking.
I also advised the patient to limit their work activities and to move their legs slightly every now and then to prevent the knee from locking up.
I instructed the patient to avoid jogging until the results of the MRI are available.
Follow-up:
I will have the nurse schedule the patient for an MRI and will follow up with the patient once the results are available.
